content: Magnesium is stored in approximately 50 to 60% of the body in bones and the remaining 40 to 50% in soft tissues.
Serum magnesium levels represent the magnesium concentration in the bloodstream, which may not reflect magnesium absorption into bones or soft tissues.
An alternative test to assess magnesium status is the red blood cell (RBC) magnesium test, which measures magnesium in red blood cells, reflecting magnesium levels in bone marrow.
Decreased RBC magnesium levels indicate potential magnesium deficiency and suggest the body is pulling magnesium from bones and soft tissues to maintain serum levels.
A comprehensive approach to assessing magnesium status includes both serum magnesium levels and RBC magnesium tests, along with considering clinical symptoms of magnesium deficiency.
